// SonarLint scope: Takes effect when THIS folder is the open VS Code workspace (File > Open Folder > Oneops-NextGen V1). SonarLint then analyzes only this project; these globs drop generated/vendor/data noise so the Problems panel shows only our code.
sonarlint.analysisExcludesStandalone: **/.venv/**, **/__pycache__/**, **/*.pyc, **/*_pb2.py, **/*_pb2_grpc.py, proto/**, contracts/**, data/**, docs/**, langgraph_docs/**, registries/**, ops/**, **/node_modules/**, src/oneops/api/static/**
// extraPaths: src/ = first-party layout. The .venv site-packages line forces Pylance to resolve installed deps (structlog/opentelemetry/…) for EVERY file even if the selected interpreter is wrong — so no 'could not be resolved' anywhere. database/ resolves the per-service loader helpers (_lib._loader / _lib._worker_base) imported via a runtime sys.path insert. (python3.12 matches this project's venv.)
// diagnosticMode: Analyse the WHOLE project (not just open files), so all issues show in the Problems panel at once — no clicking file-by-file.
// typeCheckingMode: Match the CI type authority (mypy), not Pyright's stricter defaults — so the editor doesn't flag 100+ things the CI gate passes.
